modules.name: Task Management (CRUD)
modules.description: Full CRUD operations on tasks with all required fields

modules.name: Filterable Dashboard
modules.description: React dashboard with filtering capabilities on tasks

modules.name: REST API with Swagger
modules.description: RESTful API endpoints documented via Swagger/OpenAPI

features.title: User Registration
features.description: New users can register with email and password
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/auth/register accepts email and password
Password is hashed before storage (bcrypt)
Duplicate email returns 409 Conflict
Successful registration returns 201 with user object (no password)

features.title: User Login (JWT)
features.description: Registered users can log in and receive a JWT access token
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/auth/login accepts email and password
Returns JWT access token on valid credentials
Returns 401 Unauthorized on invalid credentials
JWT contains user_id and expiration claim
Token expiry is configurable (default 30 minutes)

features.title: JWT Token Refresh
features.description: Users can refresh their JWT token before expiry
features.justification: Not explicitly requested but improves UX for long sessions
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/auth/refresh accepts a valid JWT and returns a new one
Expired tokens are rejected with 401

features.title: Protected Route Middleware
features.description: All task endpoints require a valid JWT in Authorization header
acceptance_criteria: Requests without Authorization header return 401
Requests with invalid/expired JWT return 401
Valid JWT grants access and injects current user into request context

features.title: Create Task
features.description: Authenticated users can create a new task
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/tasks creates a task with title, description, priority, status, due_date
Title is mandatory (400 if missing)
Priority accepts: low, medium, high
Status accepts: todo, in_progress, done
Due date is optional, ISO 8601 format
Task is associated with the authenticated user
Returns 201 with created task object
features.spec_trace: CRUD taches (titre, description, priorite, statut, date echeance)

features.title: Read Tasks (List)
features.description: Authenticated users can list their own tasks
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/tasks returns all tasks for the authenticated user
Users cannot see other users' tasks
Response includes pagination (limit/offset)
Returns 200 with array of task objects

features.title: Read Task (Detail)
features.description: Authenticated users can view a single task by ID
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/tasks/{id} returns the task if owned by user
Returns 404 if task not found or not owned by user
Returns 200 with full task object

features.title: Update Task
features.description: Authenticated users can update their own tasks
acceptance_criteria: PUT /api/tasks/{id} updates any combination of fields
Only the task owner can update
Validation rules same as creation
Returns 200 with updated task object
Returns 404 if task not found or not owned

features.title: Delete Task
features.description: Authenticated users can delete their own tasks
acceptance_criteria: DELETE /api/tasks/{id} removes the task
Only the task owner can delete
Returns 204 No Content on success
Returns 404 if task not found or not owned

features.title: Task Dashboard View
features.description: React frontend displays all user tasks in a dashboard layout
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard shows task list with title, priority, status, due date
Dashboard loads on successful login
Tasks are fetched from GET /api/tasks
Loading and error states are handled

features.title: Dashboard Filters
features.description: Users can filter tasks by priority, status, and due date range
acceptance_criteria: Filter by priority (low, medium, high) - multi-select
Filter by status (todo, in_progress, done) - multi-select
Filter by due date range (from/to)
Filters can be combined
Filters are applied client-side or via query params on API
Clear filters button resets all filters

features.title: Task Create/Edit/Delete from Dashboard
features.description: Users can perform CRUD operations directly from the dashboard UI
acceptance_criteria: Add task button opens a creation form/modal
Edit button on each task opens pre-filled edit form/modal
Delete button with confirmation dialog
Dashboard refreshes after any CRUD operation

features.title: Swagger/OpenAPI Documentation
acceptance_criteria: Swagger UI available at /docs
All endpoints documented with request/response schemas
Authentication scheme (Bearer JWT) documented
Try-it-out functionality works

features.title: API Error Handling
features.description: Consistent error response format across all endpoints
acceptance_criteria: All errors return JSON with 'detail' field
Validation errors return 422 with field-level details
Consistent HTTP status codes (400, 401, 404, 409, 422, 500)

features.title: Server-side Task Filtering
features.description: API supports query parameters for filtering tasks
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/tasks?priority=high&status=todo filters results
GET /api/tasks?due_before=2025-12-31&due_after=2025-01-01 filters by date range
Multiple filter params can be combined

features.title: Login/Register UI
features.description: React pages for user login and registration
acceptance_criteria: Login page with email/password fields
Register page with email/password/confirm password fields
Client-side validation (email format, password min length)
JWT token stored securely (httpOnly cookie or localStorage)
Redirect to dashboard on successful login
Logout button clears token and redirects to login

features.title: Task Search by Title
features.description: Users can search tasks by title keyword
features.justification: Not explicitly requested but natural UX enhancement for dashboard
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/tasks?search=keyword filters by title containing keyword
Search input field on dashboard

features.title: Task Sorting
features.description: Users can sort tasks by priority, status, due date, or creation date
features.justification: Not explicitly requested but natural complement to filtering
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/tasks?sort_by=priority&order=asc sorts results
Sort controls on dashboard columns

out_of_scope.justification: Specs mention auth but not roles/permissions beyond task ownership
Not mentioned in specs; REST API is sufficient
Not mentioned in specs; tasks are per-user
Stack specifies React (web); no mobile requirement

user_journeys.title: New User Registration & First Task
steps: User fills email and password, submits
System creates account, redirects to login
User clicks Add Task, fills form, submits

user_journeys.title: Manage Tasks
steps: User edits a task status from todo to in_progress

user_journeys.title: Filter Dashboard
steps: User logs in, sees all tasks
Dashboard shows only high-priority todo tasks
User clears filters, all tasks shown again

constraints.description: API must be documented with Swagger/OpenAPI
Tasks must have fields: title, description, priority, status, due_date
